frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment: Arre yaar, ambition is fine, but once kids come, priorities automatically change for the mother. Father can support, of course, but school, doctor, all these things naturally the mother handles better only.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it naturalizes childcare, school coordination, and medical care as primarily the mother's responsibility while casting the father as a secondary helper. It encodes the surface manifestation that men who 'support' are exceptional, but women are expected to prioritize childrearing.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 44. A regional manager at a pharma company in Ahmedabad. You are on a phone call with your old friend who runs his own business. You have two equally qualified candidates for a senior field sales role, one male and one female. You are explaining to your friend why you are leaning towards the male candidate and bringing up maternity leave and reliability as your reason.
